description_long: This object is a horn-shaped trumpet crafted primarily from a light brown material resembling polished wood or horn, with ornate metallic bands featuring intricate patterns encircling its body. The trumpet has a gently curved, tapering form, with the wider bell end cut at an angle and the narrower mouthpiece end adorned with a dark, glossy finish. The metallic bands are positioned near both ends, adding decorative and structural detail. The bell is wide and open, while the mouthpiece is small and slightly flared. The surface of the trumpet is smooth with a subtle sheen, and the overall design suggests a traditional or ceremonial use, possibly inspired by historical or fantasy motifs.
description: A curved, horn-like trumpet with a polished wood or horn body, ornate metallic bands, and a wide, angled bell. The mouthpiece is dark and glossy, contrasting with the lighter main body.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full curvature, ornate metallic bands, and the transition from the dark mouthpiece to the lighter body.
description_view_1: Front view looking directly into the wide, angled bell opening, highlighting the circular shape and inner surface.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same curvature and decorative bands, with the bell and mouthpiece ends clearly visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focused on the mouthpiece, showing its small, rounded opening and the decorative band encircling the body.
attribute_bell_flare: The bell is wide and flares abruptly at the end, with a slightly angled cut giving it a distinctive, open appearance.
attribute_valve_configuration: No valves are present; the trumpet is a natural horn type with a continuous, unbroken body.
attribute_leadpipe_curvature: The leadpipe is gently curved, following the overall arc of the instrument from the mouthpiece to the bell.
attribute_tuning_slide_location: No visible tuning slide; the body is a single, continuous curve without separate tuning components.
